content: I have two accounts. I turned off unified inbox. But now when I open T-bird it shows me the wrong inbox. How do I set my main one as the default?
creator: ptrdahl11

content:
Peter Dahl said
But now when I open T-bird it shows me the wrong inbox. How do I set my main one as the default?

The following steps might be helpful: "Settings" > "Accounts" section > press and hold the two-horizontal-bar icon on the right hand side of your main email account > drag and drop the two-horizontal-bar icon (and the corresponding email account) to the top of the list of email accounts > close Thunderbird for Android > start Thunderbird for Android again.
creator: Yu5tiqX9og

content:
1. "Settings" > "Accounts" section > tap the account you want to configure.

2. "Sending mail" > "Outgoing server".

2.1. If "Authentication" is "Normal password": change the password in "Password" > tap "Next".

2.2. If "Authentication" is "OAuth 2.0": tap "Next" > (I haven't tested it, but I think what will happen is:) you will be asked to log in to your email account. Use your username and new password to log in.

content:
No such thing for Android. Thunderbird for Android is designed for mobile screens, and table view does not fit.

Also, Thunderbird for Android is not a port (modified version) of Thunderbird for Desktop, therefore it doesn't have many desktop features.

content: Currently, Thunderbird mobile only supports plain text signatures and does not allow HTML or images. A possible workaround is to manually insert the image URL in the signature or use a third-party email app that supports HTML signatures. Hopefully, Thunderbird will add this feature in the future!

content:
1. "Settings" > "Accounts" section > tap the account you want to configure.

2. "Fetching mail" > "Incoming server".

2.1. If "Authentication" is "Normal password": change the password in "Password" > tap "Next".

2.2. If "Authentication" is "OAuth 2.0": tap "Next" > (I haven't tested it, but I think what will happen is:) you will be asked to log in to your email account. Use your username and new password to log in.

3. Repeat step 1.

4. "Sending mail" > "Outgoing server".

4.1. If "Authentication" is "Normal password": repeat step 2.1.

4.2. If "Authentication" is "OAuth 2.0": repeat step 2.2.

content:
Through trial and error, I finally found the solution to my question.

Go to Settings, select the desired account name, then select "Folders." You will see "auto-expand," then "show only subscribed," then a list of folder names. Most of them will say automatic in italics underneath the name.

Find "Sent folder," click on it, and a popup will be displayed, allowing you to choose a folder name. Unselect Automatic (None) and select Sent. There is no OK button, so go back using the back navigation button, not the cancel button.
